contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: iching_oracle QMDJ panel polish, two-PR cluster on TrueSightDAO/oracle. PR #11: reordered panels so Explore-64 sits directly under the I-Ching results and QMDJ panel sits between Explore-64 and DAO Advisory (cast-result flow now reads I-Ching results -> Explore the 64 hexagrams -> QMDJ -> DAO Advisory); added a 'What time-basis is this chart?' glossary section clarifying that the cast is 時家奇門 (hour-based, anchored to 時柱, rotating per 2-hour shichen) and explaining how to consult multiple times a day by simply recasting at each question. PR #12: fixed a horizontal-overflow bug where long paragraphs and wide reference tables were getting clipped because the .reference-panel parent uses overflow:hidden — added grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) to .qmdj-glossary-body, overflow-wrap: anywhere on glossary paragraphs, and wrapped each .qmdj-ref-table in a .qmdj-table-scroll div with overflow-x: auto so wide tables scroll horizontally inside the panel; added a 'Why some palaces have a bold outline' legend above the Nine Palaces table explaining the visual cue (outer ring = 值符 / focal point, inner ring = 值使 / active expression, double ring = both in the same palace = primary signal). Also clarified in conversation that day-based 日家奇門 is a structurally different framework (different rules for which star is 值符 / which door is 值使) and would need a separate engine if a toggle is wanted.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
--------
